user_request: Prepare a detailed plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000/- advanced as a hand loan by bank transfer on 15-March-2022. Defendant is Ramesh Kumar, residing at 45, MG Road, Bengaluru-560001. Plaintiff is Suresh Patel, residing at 22, Indiranagar, Bengaluru-560038. There is no written agreement. The transaction is supported by bank statement (HDFC Bank, A/c 1234567890, UTR HDFC220315000001) and WhatsApp communications. The Defendant has failed to repay despite repeated oral demands and legal notice dated 10-January-2023. The cause of action arose on the date of loan and on each refusal to repay. Plead limitation properly from date of default. Claim interest at 12% per annum from date of loan till realization. Draft strictly in accordance with Order VII CPC. Include proper cause of action (transaction date + default date + continuing), jurisdiction, valuation, ad valorem court fee and prayer clause. Use formal Indian court language suitable for filing before City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

facts.summary: Recovery of Rs.15,00,000/- advanced as hand loan via bank transfer on 15-March-2022. Defendant failed to repay despite repeated oral demands and legal notice dated 10-January-2023. No written agreement exists.

Statutory notice prior to filing suit

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint for Recovery of Hand Loan under Order VII CPC
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU
MONEY SUIT NO. ____ OF {{YEAR}}

Suresh Patel,
Age: {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}},
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at 22, Indiranagar, Bengaluru-560038.
... Plaintiff

Versus

Ramesh Kumar,
Age: {{DEFENDANT_AGE}},
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at 45, MG Road, Bengaluru-560001.
... Defendant

PLAINT UNDER ORDER VII OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

1. That the Plaintiff is an adult Indian citizen residing at the address mentioned in the cause title and is competent to contract and sue.

2. That the Defendant is an adult Indian citizen residing at the address mentioned in the cause title and is competent to contract and be sued.

3. That on or about 15th March 2022, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff and requested a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) for his personal purposes. The Plaintiff, being well acquainted with the Defendant and trusting his solemn assurance to repay, agreed to advance the said loan amount to the Defendant.

4. That pursuant to the said oral agreement and request, the Plaintiff transferred the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant's account via electronic bank transfer (RTGS/NEFT) from his HDFC Bank Account No. 1234567890, bearing Unique Transaction Reference (UTR) No. HDFC220315000001, dated 15th March 2022. No written agreement, promissory note, or acknowledgment of debt was executed between the parties in respect of the said loan transaction, and the same rests purely upon oral understanding and trust between the parties.

5. That the said loan was advanced on the clear oral understanding between the parties that the Defendant would repay the same on demand within a reasonable time. Alternatively, the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the principal amount despite repeated oral demands made by the Plaintiff from time to time after the expiry of reasonable time.

6. That the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- or any part thereof despite repeated oral demands made by the Plaintiff thereafter, and particularly despite a legal notice dated 10th January 2023 sent by the Plaintiff through his Advocate demanding repayment of the said amount within the stipulated time, which notice was received by the Defendant but failed to comply with or make any payment towards the outstanding dues.

7. CAUSE OF ACTION:
   (a) The original cause of action arose on 15th March 2022, when the Plaintiff advanced the loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant via bank transfer and the Defendant became liable to repay the same;
   (b) The cause of action further accrued and continues to accrue from the date(s) of default and refusal by the Defendant to repay the said amount upon oral demands made by the Plaintiff from time to time, and specifically from {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEFAULT_OR_REFUSAL}} when the Defendant first refused to repay the amount despite specific demand, rendering the liability to pay immediate and absolute;
   (c) The cause of action is continuing in nature as the Defendant continues to withhold the Plaintiff's money wrongfully and unlawfully, and the liability to repay the principal along with interest remains outstanding and subsisting.

8. LIMITATION:
   That the present suit has been filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, read with Section 3 thereof. The right to sue first accrued to the Plaintiff from the date of default/refusal by the Defendant to repay the amount upon demand, that is, from {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEFAULT_OR_REFUSAL}}, and the present suit is filed within {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}} from the said date when the right to sue accrued, and is therefore well within limitation and not barred by time.

9. JURISDICTION:
   (a) This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain the present suit as the value of the subject-matter is Rs. 15,00,000/-, which is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of the City Civil Court, Bengaluru;
   (b) This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the Defendant resides and carries on business within the jurisdiction of this Court at 45, MG Road, Bengaluru-560001, and part of the cause of action has arisen within the jurisdiction of this Court.

10. VALUATION AND COURT FEE:
    The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. The Plaintiff has paid ad valorem court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (Rupees {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT_WORDS}} only) computed in accordance with the Karnataka Court-Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, and the First Schedule thereto, being Rs. 6,560/- for the first Rs. 4,00,000/- plus Rs. 50/- for every Rs. 5,000/- or part thereof in excess of Rs. 4,00,000/-.

11. RELIEF CLAIMED:
    The Plaintiff claims the following reliefs against the Defendant:
    (a) A decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) being the principal amount of the hand loan advanced to the Defendant;
    (b) Interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the said principal amount from the date of loan (15th March 2022) till the date of realization/decree;
    (c) Costs of the suit;
    (d) Such other relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

12. EVIDENCE:
    The Plaintiff shall rely upon the following documents and evidence in support of his case:
    (a) Bank statement/transfer record of HDFC Bank Account No. 1234567890 evidencing transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- dated 15th March 2022 bearing UTR No. HDFC220315000001 - Annexure-A;
    (b) Printouts of WhatsApp communications and chat history between the Plaintiff and Defendant regarding the loan transaction, acknowledgment of receipt, and subsequent demands for repayment - Annexure-B;
    (c) Copy of legal notice dated 10th January 2023 sent to the Defendant demanding repayment {{AND_ACKNOWLEDGMENT}} - Annexure-C;
    (d) Such other oral and documentary evidence as may be permitted by this Hon'ble Court at the time of trial.

13. That the Plaintiff states that certificates under Section 65B of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872, shall be filed at the time of trial in respect of the electronic records and computer output forming part of Annexures-A and B, as required by law for the admissibility of electronic evidence.

14. That the Plaintiff does not wish to claim any exemption from the law of limitation.

PRAYER

In view of the foregoing facts and submissions, it is most humbly prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) together with interest thereon at the rate of 12% per annum from 15th March 2022 till the date of realization/decree against the Defendant;
(b) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
(c) Pass such other order or direction as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

Place: Bengaluru
Date: {{DATE_OF_FILING}}

(Suresh Patel)
Plaintiff

VERIFICATION

I, Suresh Patel, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify and state on solemn affirmation that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 11 of the above plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge, and those of paragraphs 12 to 14 are based on legal advice and believed to be true.

Verified at Bengaluru on this {{VERIFICATION_DATE}} day of {{VERIFICATION_MONTH}}, {{VERIFICATION_YEAR}}.

(Suresh Patel)
Plaintiff

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

1. Annexure-A: Copy of HDFC Bank Statement/Transfer Record dated 15th March 2022 evidencing transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- bearing UTR HDFC220315000001;
2. Annexure-B: Printouts of WhatsApp communications between Plaintiff and Defendant regarding loan transaction and repayment demands;
3. Annexure-C: Copy of Legal Notice dated 10th January 2023 {{ACKNOWLEDGMENT_DETAIL}}.

Place: Bengaluru
Date: {{DATE_OF_FILING}}

(Suresh Patel)
Plaintiff

That the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- or any part thereof despite repeated oral demands made by the Plaintiff thereafter, and particularly despite a legal notice dated 10th January 2023 sent by the Plaintiff through his Advocate demanding repayment of the said amount within the stipulated time, which notice was received by the Defendant but failed to comply with or make any payment towards the outstanding dues.

7. CAUSE OF ACTION:
   (a) The original cause of action arose on 15th March 2022, when the Plaintiff advanced the loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant via bank transfer and the Defendant became liable to repay the same;
   (b) The cause of action further accrued and continues to accrue from the date(s) of default and refusal by the Defendant to repay the said amount upon oral demands made by the Plaintiff from time to time, and specifically from {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEFAULT_OR_REFUSAL}} when the Defendant first refused to repay the amount despite specific demand, rendering the liability to pay immediate and absolute;
   (c) The cause of action is continuing in nature as the Defendant continues to withhold the Plaintiff's money wrongfully and unlawfully, and the liability to repay the principal along with interest remains outstanding and subsisting.

8. LIMITATION:
   That the present suit has been filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, read with Section 3 thereof. The right to sue first accrued to the Plaintiff from the date of default/refusal by the Defendant to repay the amount upon demand, that is, from {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEFAULT_OR_REFUSAL}}, and the present suit is filed within {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}} from the said date when the right to sue accrued, and is therefore well within limitation and not barred by time.

9. JURISDICTION:
   (a) This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain the present suit as the value of the subject-matter is Rs. 15,00,000/-, which is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of the City Civil Court, Bengaluru;
   (b) This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the Defendant resides and carries on business within the jurisdiction of this Court at 45, MG Road, Bengaluru-560001, and part of the cause of action has arisen within the jurisdiction of this Court.

10. VALUATION AND COURT FEE:
    The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. The Plaintiff has paid ad valorem court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (Rupees {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT_WORDS}} only) computed in accordance with the Karnataka Court-Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, and the First Schedule thereto, being Rs. 6,560/- for the first Rs. 4,00,000/- plus Rs. 50/- for every Rs. 5,000/- or part thereof in excess of Rs. 4,00,000/-.

11. RELIEF CLAIMED:
    The Plaintiff claims the following reliefs against the Defendant:
    (a) A decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) being the principal amount of the hand loan advanced to the Defendant;
    (b) Interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the said principal amount from the date of loan (15th March 2022) till the date of realization/decree;
    (c) Costs of the suit;
    (d) Such other relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

12. EVIDENCE:
    The Plaintiff shall rely upon the following documents and evidence in support of his case:
    (a) Bank statement/transfer record of HDFC Bank Account No. 1234567890 evidencing transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- dated 15th March 2022 bearing UTR No. HDFC220315000001 - Annexure-A;
    (b) Printouts of WhatsApp communications and chat history between the Plaintiff and Defendant regarding the loan transaction, acknowledgment of receipt, and subsequent demands for repayment - Annexure-B;
    (c) Copy of legal notice dated 10th January 2023 sent to the Defendant demanding repayment {{AND_ACKNOWLEDGMENT}} - Annexure-C;
    (d) Such other oral and documentary evidence as may be permitted by this Hon'ble Court at the time of trial.

13. That the Plaintiff does not wish to claim any exemption from the law of limitation.

PRAYER

In view of the foregoing facts and submissions, it is most humbly prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) together with interest thereon at the rate of 12% per annum from 15th March 2022 till the date of realization/decree against the Defendant;
(b) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
(c) Pass such other order or direction as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Claim for pre-suit interest at 12% per annum from date of loan without written agreement specifying interest rate may be legally unsustainable; pre-suit interest generally requires contractual stipulation or statutory basis

The Plaintiff, being well acquainted with the Defendant and trusting his solemn assurance to repay, agreed to advance the said loan amount to the Defendant.

4. No written agreement, promissory note, or acknowledgment of debt was executed between the parties in respect of the said loan transaction, and the same rests purely upon oral understanding and trust between the parties.

5. That the said loan was advanced on the clear oral understanding between the parties that the Defendant would repay the same on demand within a reasonable time. Alternatively, the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the principal amount despite repeated oral demands made by the Plaintiff from time to time after the expiry of reasonable time.

6. That the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- or any part thereof despite repeated oral demands made by the Plaintiff thereafter, and particularly despite a legal notice dated 10th January 2023 sent by the Plaintiff through his Advocate demanding repayment of the said amount within the stipulated time, which notice was received by the Defendant but failed to comply with or make any payment towards the outstanding dues.

7. CAUSE OF ACTION:
   (a) The original cause of action arose on 15th March 2022, when the Plaintiff advanced the loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant via bank transfer and the Defendant became liable to repay the same;
   (b) The cause of action further accrued and continues to accrue from the date(s) of default and refusal by the Defendant to repay the said amount upon oral demands made by the Plaintiff from time to time, and specifically from {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEFAULT_OR_REFUSAL}} when the Defendant first refused to repay the amount despite specific demand, rendering the liability to pay immediate and absolute;
   (c) The cause of action is continuing in nature as the Defendant continues to withhold the Plaintiff's money wrongfully and unlawfully, and the liability to repay the principal along with interest remains outstanding and subsisting.

8. LIMITATION:
   That the present suit has been filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, read with Section 3 thereof. The right to sue first accrued to the Plaintiff from the date of default/refusal by the Defendant to repay the amount upon demand, that is, from {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEFAULT_OR_REFUSAL}}, and the present suit is filed within {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}} from the said date when the right to sue accrued, and is therefore well within limitation and not barred by time.

9. JURISDICTION:
   (a) This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain the present suit as the value of the subject-matter is Rs. 15,00,000/-, which is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of the City Civil Court, Bengaluru;
   (b) This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the Defendant resides and carries on business within the jurisdiction of this Court at 45, MG Road, Bengaluru-560001, and part of the cause of action has arisen within the jurisdiction of this Court.

10. VALUATION AND COURT FEE:
    The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. The Plaintiff has paid ad valorem court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (Rupees {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT_WORDS}} only) computed in accordance with the Karnataka Court-Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, and the First Schedule thereto, being Rs. 6,560/- for the first Rs. 4,00,000/- plus Rs. 50/- for every Rs. 5,000/- or part thereof in excess of Rs. 4,00,000/-.

11. RELIEF CLAIMED:
    The Plaintiff claims the following reliefs against the Defendant:
    (a) A decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) being the principal amount of the hand loan advanced to the Defendant;
    (b) Interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the said principal amount from the date of loan (15th March 2022) till the date of realization/decree;
    (c) Costs of the suit;
    (d) Such other relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

12. EVIDENCE:
    The Plaintiff shall rely upon the following documents and evidence in support of his case:
    (a) Bank statement/transfer record of HDFC Bank Account No. 1234567890 evidencing transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- dated 15th March 2022 bearing UTR No. HDFC220315000001 - Annexure-A;
    (b) Printouts of WhatsApp communications and chat history between the Plaintiff and Defendant regarding the loan transaction, acknowledgment of receipt, and subsequent demands for repayment - Annexure-B;
    (c) Copy of legal notice dated 10th January 2023 sent to the Defendant demanding repayment {{AND_ACKNOWLEDGMENT}} - Annexure-C;
    (d) Such other oral and documentary evidence as may be permitted by this Hon'ble Court at the time of trial.

13. That the Plaintiff states that certificates under Section 65B of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872, shall be filed at the time of trial in respect of the electronic records and computer output forming part of Annexures-A and B, as required by law for the admissibility of electronic evidence.

14. That the Plaintiff does not wish to claim any exemption from the law of limitation.

PRAYER

In view of the foregoing facts and submissions, it is most humbly prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) together with interest thereon at the rate of 12% per annum from 15th March 2022 till the date of realization/decree against the Defendant;
(b) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
